question_text: A syringe contains 60 cm³ of gas at a pressure of 100 kPa. The plunger is pushed so the volume becomes 30 cm³. Assuming temperature stays constant, what is the new pressure? (Use Boyle's law: P₁V₁ = P₂V₂)
explanation: Using Boyle's law: P₁V₁ = P₂V₂, so 100 × 60 = P₂ × 30. Therefore P₂ = 6000 ÷ 30 = 200 kPa. When volume halves, pressure doubles.
